description: Locate specific buttons and tags hidden on the webpage.

slides.title: The Old Way 🏛️
slides.content:
## By ID and Class

For 20 years, developers exclusively used two main methods to find things:

1. `document.getElementById("login")`: Finds exactly 1 unique element that has `id="login"`.
2. `document.getElementsByClassName("card")`: Finds a massive Array of EVERY element that uses the `class="card"`.

These methods still work perfectly, but they are a bit clunky and only let you search by one specific attribute.

slides.title: The Modern Way ✨

quizData.question: What is the primary difference in how you write standard `document.getElementById("box")` versus the modern `document.querySelector("#box")`?

options.text: querySelector requires standard CSS notation like '#' or '.' to know what it is looking for.

quizData.explanation: Because querySelector is a highly flexible multi-tool, you MUST provide explicit CSS symbols (. for class, # for ID) so it knows exactly what to look for.

slides.title: Finding Multiple Items 📦

quizData.question:
If you have 10 `

` tags on your website, and you run `let text = document.querySelector("p");`, what is saved in the variable?
quizData.explanation: Standard querySelector() grabs the FIRST matching item and immediately stops searching. To get all 10, you must use querySelectorAll()!
